full_text:
An unusual heat wave brings record March temperatures to the Bay Area this week, with highs expected to reach 90 degrees through Friday in the East Bay. Students at Cal State East Bay are finding ways to stay cool amid the unseasonably high temperatures, including seeking shade and wearing shorts and short sleeves. The National Weather Service warns that the extreme heat is due to an early-season high-pressure system, and advises residents to stay hydrated, seek air-conditioned spaces, and never leave children or pets unattended in vehicles.
